title: [有幸] 參議員 Hawley: 有幸與反抗暴力獨裁政權既香港人曾經站係同一條街

replies.msg:
係通過香港自治法案既同時，岩岩 Sen. Hawley 成功係參議院 通過一份屌鳩中共推匪安法既resolution
係發言當中，hawley 強調自已曾經黎過香港，有幸同反抗呢個暴力同獨裁政權既香港人曾經一齊站係同一條街上面
Hawley 強調，今日參議院希望帶比所有香港人 － 希望，hope，美國和全世界的自由人民將與香港人（The free people of this nation and the world are with you) , 亦再次強調美國唔會完全冇行動

認真，真係聽到眼濕濕，得1分半鐘，但真係好感動，希望幫大家回下血

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CdMWtTjukeE

We are ready
Sir, this way
Josh Hawley 真係好靚仔
josh

係發言當中，hawley 強調自已曾經黎過香港，有幸同反抗呢個暴力同獨裁政權既香港人曾經一齊站係同一條街上面
Hawley 強調，今日參議院希望帶比所有香港人 － 希望，hope，美國和全世界的自由人民將與香港人（The free people of this nation and the world are with you) , 亦再次強調美國唔會完全冇行動

replies.msg:
Hawley d speech 次次都係好撚正
我會去佢website搵返transcript丁學英文

quote.msg: Josh Hawley 真係好靚仔

replies.msg: 好正

hawley 推動既resolution 全文：

That it is the sense of the Senate that—

(1) the Hong Kong national security law proposed by the Government of the People’s Republic of China would violate the legal obligations of that government under—

(A) the 1984 Sino-British Joint Declaration, which guarantees for 50 years the protection of the basic rights and freedoms of the people of Hong Kong, including those of speech, press, assembly, association, travel, movement, correspondence, and strike; and

(B) the Hong Kong Basic Law, which reserves the authority for enacting laws prohibiting treason, secession, sedition, subversion, and foreign interference to the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region; and

(2) the United States Government should use all diplomatic means available, including targeted sanctions, to—

(A) dissuade the Government of the People’s Republic of China from passing the proposed Hong Kong national security law;

(B) compel the Government of the People’s Republic of China to rescind the proposed Hong Kong national security law, if it is passed; and

(C) rally all free nations to stand with the people of Hong Kong against increasingly severe violations by the Government of the People’s Republic of China of the rights and liberties guaranteed to them under the 1984 Sino-British Joint Declaration and the Hong Kong Basic Law.

https://www.congress.gov/bill/116th-congress/senate-resolution/596/text?r=13&s=1

(A) the 1984 Sino-British Joint Declaration, which guarantees for 50 years the protection of the basic rights and freedoms of the people of Hong Kong, including those of speech, press, assembly, association, travel, movement, correspondence, and strike; and
